chunks.text: The Master of Science (M.S.) and Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) programs emphasize research preparation and experience. Please note that Berkeley EECS no longer requires, nor accepts, GRE scores. The Fall 2026 Admission application is now closed. The next available application cycle will be for Fall 2027. The application will reopen in September 2026.

Application prerequisites for all graduate research degree programs include: A bachelor's degree or recognized equivalent from an accredited institution. If attending a university using a 4.0 GPA scale, a minimum GPA of 3.0 (B) is required. Three letters of recommendation uploaded as PDFs. For degrees from outside the United States, see minimum degree requirements and English proficiency evidence. The GRE is no longer required nor accepted.

You can apply for the M.S., M.S./Ph.D., or Ph.D. program in either Electrical Engineering (EECS) or Computer Science (CS). EE applicants must apply to EECS, and CS applicants to CS. Research areas under EE include Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences; Computer Vision and AI; Control, Intelligent Systems, Robotics; Integrated Circuits; MEMS; Physical Electronics; Signal Processing. Areas under both EE and CS include Biosystems & Computational Biology; Computer Architecture & Engineering; Cyber-Physical Systems; Energy. CS areas include AI, Database Management, Education, Graphics, HCI, Operating Systems, Programming, Scientific Computing, Security, Theory.

Admissions checklist: Open an online application. Upload required materials: Proof of English Proficiency (TOEFL/IELTS for non-English speaking countries), Statement of Purpose, Personal History Statement, 3 Letters of Recommendation (preferably from professors), Unofficial Transcripts (translated if not in English/Spanish), GPA (enter as on transcript if not 4.0 scale), Resume/CV. Pay application fee (waivers available). Submit by deadline (December 1). Late applications not accepted.

Application & Enrollment Timeline: December 1 - Online Application and Supplemental Materials Deadline. Late February & Early March - Admissions decisions sent via email. April 15 - Statement of Intent to Register (SIR) Deadline. August - Mandatory Orientations, UC Berkeley Campus. September - Applications open for next admissions cycle.

Master of Science (M.S.) Only: Full-time commitment ~2 years. Focus leads to career in industrial R&D or Ph.D. Intended mostly for current UC Berkeley Ph.D. students; admits limited exceptional applicants with research experience. Students interested in research should consider MS/PhD program. For professional master's, see Master of Engineering (M.Eng.).

Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) Only: Full-time commitment 5-6 years (starting with Bachelor's), 3-5 years (starting with Master's). Focus on research + teaching experience. Prepares for academia or industry. Offers EE and CS Ph.D. degrees. Requirements: coursework (major and two minor fields), departmental preliminary requirement (oral exam and breadth courses), qualifying exam, dissertation.

Equal Access to Application Assistance Program (EAAA): Student-run program providing guidance on application process for higher degree programs (M.S./Ph.D.). Offers feedback on statement of purpose, personal history statement, CV/resume, and other materials. Feedback is Berkeley- and admissions-focused. Participation does not guarantee admission or affect decisions.
